name: Herbed Quinoa
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Garlic clove, peeled and minced
recipeInstructions:
1. If the listed herbs are unavailable, substitute an equal amount of parsley, or 1 teaspoon dried thyme, basil, or oregano.
2. Put quinoa, water, and salt in a medium-size saucepan. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
3. Reduce heat to medium and cover partially so steam can escape. Cook at a slow boil until grains have doubled in size, become translucent, and are cracked open, showing the spiral germ of each grain, about 12 minutes. If necessary, continue cooking uncovered until all liquid is absorbed or has evaporated, which may take an additional minute.
4. While quinoa cooks, combine olive oil, garlic, and a generous amount of pepper in a warmed medium-size bowl. Coarsely chop basil leaves and mix them into the olive oil mixture just before the quinoa is ready.
5. Add cooked quinoa to the olive oil and toss until thoroughly combined. Check seasonings and serve immediately.
Nice accompaniment to steamed fish or roasted meats or poultry. Serve as a main course with various vegetables and salads.
